Wetra Hound

You hand her the city pass you received, which she scrutinizes while you take the opportunity to look her over. She’s short but very... muscular, with knotted arms and thighs and even the outline of some abs between her small breasts and her chitinous girdle.

“Sorry. You may not enter,” the woman says, handing back your pass. “This is just a standard pass. Feel free to go anywhere else in Irellia’s sector - I recommend seeing The Honey Nozzle at least once, if you haven’t yet.”
“But I wanna scope the school,” you whine.
“You sure?” You look her up and down, then pat your crotch. “Well what about this pass right here?”
“I had my heart set on looking around the thollum,” you reply.
<i>“Standard?
The fuck’s

The burly myr tucks her weapon between two arms awkwardly. “Gotta have a thollum visitor’s stamp to see the thollum. See the empty spot in the bottom-left? Nothin’ there,” she returns, and then to head off any further discussion: “If you think your pass was restricted in error, best see the office that issued it.”
That’ll mean Lyralla. You say your goodbyes.
“Have a good day,” the guard says.
Next

You hand over the card and take the opportunity to look them over while she reads it. The right one is ordinary-looking, while the shorter left one can only be described as fit, with slightly-visible abs and thewy, knotted limbs tucked into chitin sleeves. Her well-defined bicep uncurls as she hands the pass back.

The other nods, and you are led into the thollum. The foyer that you enter is very large, with a wide open floor flanked by unexpected columns, capped ovoids in a surprisingly Doric variant of the myr style. Gold myr of many shapes and ages, all female, come and go through these arteries. The cavern is discolored and distressed inside as well, though instead of the natural chips of the exterior stones, the interior has clearly been explored by hundreds of thousands of small, curious hands - the perfect forms and clean lines of proud craftsmen are still evident where the walls and fixtures meet the ceiling, up where children and their colored chalks cannot reach. Despite the eye-level disfigurements, the sheer scale and impressive, temple-like plan fill you with a sense of being somewhere immeasurably sophisticated and important.

“Big, isn’t it?” asks your companion. A note of pride sounds in her voice. Well, this is probably her alma mater, after all. “Did you have a particular place you needed to be, or are you just here to see the grounds?”
“Hey, [pc.name],” says the well-built woman at the entrance. “Back to visit again?” She checks you in while her partner radios your arrival to whichever desk they report to.
Yarasta is most likely in her chamber at the foot of the stairs, one floor below. If you want to visit, you’ll need to ask a guard to deliver her a message.
The myr guard smiles

The chittering of a nearby voice rouses you from a fitful slumber. You reach upward to rub at your eyes only to find your movement impeded by several straps tightly wound across your body. A pit forms in your stomach as awareness of your surroundings finally percolates through your mental haze: you are strapped to an operating table in the middle of a medbay. Wires trail from a coif-like device affixed to your head and upper back, feeding information to a bank of glowing screens lining the walls. “Oh? finally awake?” Your attention snaps to a figure whom you instantly recognize as Velika. Her lips crease into a wicked grin as she caresses your cheek with one hand. “I was wondering if we’d get a chance to talk.” A cold trickle of sweat works its way down your brow as your stomach knots itself in fear. “What the hell did you do to me?” you ask while tensing at your bonds. “Nothing yet. Relax, innie.” You wince as her dainty fingers trace a path down to your neck. “Soon this will all just be a bad dream — a figment of your overactive imagination.” Questions race through the corridors of your mind, threatening to overwhelm you before you finally give voice to the most basic one: why? “Why?” The cyborg chuckles mirthlessly. “You mean you don’t know? You’re a weapon, [pc.name].” “What?” “Those nanomachines in your blood aren’t just for curing diseases. They’re just a few modifications away from being a bioweapon: the newest iteration in the galactic arms race.” Velika watches you closely as she explains this, her robotic eyes searching your face. “It’s winner-takes-all when it comes to politics. I’m just making sure the outer rim can defend itself from the likes of you.” “That’s insane,” you reply, vehemently denying being part of any sort of conspiracy against the ‘rim. “You might not have it in for us. But Steele Tech does. It’s part of the core world imperial regime. You can’t afford to let us wriggle out from under you. Literally. Your father made his fortune by exploiting the mineral wealth of the ‘rim, after all.” The cyborg runs her fingers [pc.hasHair |through your [pc.hair] |across the top of your head ]. Pain throbs downward from the back of your skull, emptying your world of everything save for Velika’s gleaming prosthetic eyes and the mechanical bite of the brain probe. You can feel the alien logic of the machine battering incessantly at your mind, methodically erasing your consciousness through its relentless interrogation. The cyborg smirks, straddling you so that her legs are on either side of your hips before leaning forward, her breath ghosting against your [pc.skin] as she speaks. “It’ll all be over soon, innie.” You can feel a swirling in your psyche, the pain coursing down your spine giving way to a twisted, feverish desire for her. “Just relax. I’ll take good care of you.” Her hair falls like a curtain around you as she favors you with a triumphant smirk. White static claws at the edge of your vision as your body thrums with artificially-induced ecstasy, threatening to overwhelm you. As you close your eyes, you hear a whispering female voice. Velika’s voice. Your mind wakes in bliss. Prosthetic fingers slip beneath the waistband of your fatigues, fingers gliding through the slick, swollen folds of your pussy. Your fingers begin to pulse and vibrate against your sex. “By far the best aug I ever paid for,” you think to yourself, a soft, contented moan escaping from your lips. When was the last time you were unable to unwind like this? You can’t remember. The monotony of garrison life makes each day blur into the next. Your mind drifts towards the image of taunt uniforms stretching across the chests of the new conscripts as you delve deeper into yourself, the mechanical stimulation of your digits sending bolts of pleasure shooting throughout your core. You won’t be seeing much of them in the near future. But who cares? Being selected to go abroad... now that’s something. Your free hand comes up to maul your own breasts as your sex clenches and flutters around your intruding fingers, gripping them like a hot, wet fist. Hardly anyone gets to do so these days and the damn war-scare has only made things worse. You hope you’ll be shipped off somewhere sickeningly blue, sunny, and more than a few parsecs between yourself and your old CO. As your eyes come to rest on the emblem on your livery you feel a strange tightness in your head that soon gives way to an aching throb. Dammit... you rub your temples. Why the hell did you have to get a headache now of all times? If one of your augs is on the fritz- You force yourself to look down at the insignia again before wincing, a fresh rod of pain driving its way through your skill. “Yes there’s no mistaking it... something has tripped my Infosec system. I’ll have to-” You blink, and the scene before you shifts to that of cigarette smoke and a long, rosewood table crowded by over half-a dozen technocrats. Real heavy hitters. Your chest constricts in nervousness. You feel like a damn schoolgirl, being put on the spot by a particularly overbearing teacher. But this isn’t a reprimand. You ought to feel pleased with yourself: a clap on the shoulders and a few surreptitious fingernail scrapings were all it took to get your hands on Steele’s prototype immune system. Or at least parts of it... the nanites didn’t take too well to cultivation so far. Might be bad luck. Might have been some programmed failsafe. You can’t be the only one looking to reverse engineer tech after all. Still, if what the doctors claim is true... if the technology really does have military applications... You purse your lips. You never did trust all the corporate propaganda Steele Tech churns out. Mining has always been a dirty business: blood diamonds, coups, death squads... you name it, some mining corp has tried it... or at the very least sat back and watched it happen. Still, to think that Steele Tech might turn to bioweapons... could [pc.name] really be in on such a thing? Who’s to say [pc.he] isn’t just a useful idiot? You rub your temples, groaning slightly as an insistent throbbing overtakes you. That damn machine... you never heard of a nerve stapler bouncing back on its cybernetic operator. Damn your superiors for making you go through with this. You have to cut the connection. You have to- Agony shivers through your skull as you feel a horrible tugging sensation, as if someone was trying to fish your brain out of your skull. You scream at the top of your lungs and- You bolt upright, your sweat-soaked [pc.upperGarmentOuter] hugging your frame as your eyes flit about your surroundings. Velika is nowhere to be found; a nest of fiber optic cables and an overturned monitor being the only clues of her egress. You take a deep, quieting breath before searching for your belongings, finding your [pc.gear] stashed in a pull-out body drawer in what you presume to be a morgue. You grip your [pc.weapon] as you march forward, unsure of where you are or what you might encounter.
